response.justification: Candidate has 20+ years of broad software experience and strong fundamentals (TDD, architecture, mentoring, multi-threaded systems via DLang), but the role explicitly requires a senior C++ developer for industrial automation. His only C++ experience is roughly six months at National Instruments in 2007-2008 plus a bachelor thesis project; C++ does not appear in his current skill set and his recent decade is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang. There is no evidence of MQTT, IEC 62443, message brokers, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), which are central to the role.
strengths: 20+ years of engineering experience with strong craftsmanship, TDD, and mentoring track record
Experience building high-throughput, multi-threaded, async networked services (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Familiarity with REST APIs, Node.js, and distributed/event-driven platforms (GISCollective)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over 15 years stale; not listed among current skills
No demonstrated exposure to IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial cybersecurity standards
No evidence of MQTT, message brokers, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
